content: If we map the human linguistic learning process of repeated reflective vocabulary absorption (from the language learning sense of reflexion) to the iterative self-feedback loop of LLM Self-Refine, we can build a more human-aligned refinement framework that weights reflective contextual memory over isolated output correction. This framework would train LLMs to refine outputs not just by fixing errors, but by reflecting on how well the output aligns with contextual usage patterns learned over time, similar to how language learners refine their vocabulary use through repeated reflection on context.
